text: and everything you’ve done since I met you is the total opposite .” “I did those things because I love you .” Something in her expression softened. She placed her hand on his chest, fingers curling in his coat. “You did things before you fell in love with me. Before you even knew what love was. What was your reason then ?” His processors fired through all the memories of his time with her. “You intrigued me. And…you seemed lost. On the verge of collapsing .” “You helped me at my lowest point. Maybe some of your reasons were selfish, but not all of them. You could’ve tried to demand so much of me, could’ve turned me into your slave. And even before you loved me, you gave me food, and shelter, and safety, and asked for so little in return .” “Because I had an abundance of those things to offer,” he replied. As he spoke the words, the final piece clicked into place, and he understood her anger . It must’ve shown on his face, because she smiled up at him softly and rested her forehead on his chest. “Warlord needs to be stopped, Ronin .” “I don’t think these people will risk it, Lara, but I’ll help you try to convince them all the same .” “Thank you.” She lifted her head, stood on her toes, and pressed her lips to his. He leaned into the kiss, an electric thrill pulsing over his skin. Lara wrapped her right arm around him and squeezed, only to suck in a sharp breath and step back. Lines of strain appeared around her mouth, despite her smirk. “You’d think I would remember how much that hurts. Guess I just can’t resist .” Ronin brushed the backs of his fingers over her cheek. She was still recovering, still delicate, and he missed holding her, missed feeling her body tucked against his, missed her warmth. His memories of those feelings were easily accessed, but could never compare to the real thing . He took her hand in his, and they walked together, slowly, to the room he’d been questioned in when they first arrived . Lara was quiet as they entered the stockade. Though she’d probably never seen anything like it, Ronin got the sense that she knew what the cells were for. She gave them a single glance before diverting her gaze to the concrete floor . The interrogation room — he was sure that’s what it had been, even before the Black Out — was full when Ronin and Lara walked in. They sat together in the only open seats, at the end of the table nearest the door. The base’s leadership stared at them, Colonel Rodriguez central amongst the group . Lara didn’t release Ronin’s hand . “Miss Brooks,” Rodriguez said, dipping his head slightly. “I imagine you’re acquainted with everyone here, by now .” Lara glanced around the room. “Yeah. Hi, Newton.” She raised a hand in greeting . Newton mimicked the gesture with surprising fluidity, face plates lifting into a smile. “Lara. You’re looking well .” Rodriguez cleared his throat. “We’re not here for small talk. You grew up in Cheyenne, Brooks. That means I have questions, and you’re going to answer them .” “You know, you technically all grew up in Cheyenne, too. So…you can answer them yourself .” Several of the people around the table grinned, many of whom quickly hid their mouths behind their hands . Rodriguez’s features darkened. “Stand-up comedy died when the bombs dropped .” “I don’t know what that is .” “There were people called comedians who—” Newton began, but he was silenced by a glare from the Colonel . “Look, Miss Brooks, I’m going to be frank with you. I’d appreciate it if you could cut your shit .” She opened her mouth to respond, and Ronin gave her hand a warning squeeze; he could imagine what might have come out of her mouth, rich with ‘ f ’s and hard consonants . Lara looked at Ronin and pressed her lips together. She took a slow breath, and turned back to Rodriguez. “Ronin already told you more than I ever could about Cheyenne. His mind holds every detail. Unless you plan on going after Warlord, I don’t have anything to say to you .” Multiple conversations began at once . “Enough!” Rodriguez’s gaze roamed over the others, one-at-a-time. “You’re all here as a formality. This is a matter of security, and by our laws that means I am in sole command .” The heavy silence lasted for twenty-two seconds before Rodriguez returned his attention to Lara. “Warlord has been a potential threat to this facility for decades.” He splayed his hands on the table with deliberate slowness. “Any information we can gather about him may be vital to our continued safety and functionality. But I am curious, Miss Brooks, as to why you think we’d risk our people to go after him. He gave up searching this area many years ago, and hasn’t caused us trouble since .” “Why would you not?” she asked. “If there was a wild dog living next door to you, and every now and then it killed one of your neighbor’s kids, would you just ignore it because it hadn’t done anything to you ?” “Warlord hasn’t killed any of our neigh — ” Lara pulled her hand from Ronin’s and slammed it on the table, pushing herself up. “We’re your fucking neighbors !” Ronin scanned the people with his optics; their faces bore signs of shock and shame . “We’ve been your neighbors a long fucking time, and humans and bots are both suffering because of that bastard .” “Sit down,” Rodriguez said through his teeth . “ Fu — ” “We should hear her out,” William interjected . Seconds felt like years as Rodriguez and Lara glared at one another across the table. Ronin’s fingers twitched. The tension in the air was nearly thick
